channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Megan Boswell’s attorney explains why no witnesses testified
item.description: Megan Boswell faces 19 charges, including several counts of murder, in the death of her 15-month-old daughter Evelyn.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: Canada’s new fentanyl czar says goal is to totally stop the drug from entering the US
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — The man appointed by the Canadian government to help stop fentanyl from entering the United States said his goal is to totally eliminate the drug crossing the border.
item.dc:creator: JIM MORRIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Attorney General Pam Bondi rails against New York leaders as she announces immigration lawsuit
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's newly installed attorney general, Pam Bondi, went after New York leaders Wednesday over the state's immigration policies, announcing a lawsuit in the latest effort by the Republican administration to carry out the president's hardline immigration campaign p
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: House Republicans unveil blueprint to extend $4.5 trillion in tax cuts and lift the debt ceiling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans released a budget plan Wednesday that sets the stage for advancing many of President Donald Trump's top domestic priorities, providing for up to $4.5 trillion in tax cuts and a $4 trillion increase in the debt limit so the U.S. can continue financing its bills.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New York isn't alone when it comes to granting driver's licenses to immigrants without legal status
item.description: U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi has announced legal action against New York officials over a 2019 state law allowing immigrants to obtain state-issued driver’s license regardless of legal status.
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Navy crew members eject from their jet before it crashes off the San Diego coast
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Two crew members ejected from a Navy jet before it crashed Wednesday off the San Diego coast and were quickly rescued by a sportfishing boat, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Texas county approves holding election to make SpaceX's Starbase its own city
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — A Texas county on Wednesday approved holding an election sought by SpaceX that would let residents living around billionaire Elon Musk 's company decide whether to formally create a new city called Starbase.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eastern storm cuts power to tens of thousands as California braces for flooding
item.description: Storms dumped heavy snow and freezing rain on a swath of the U.S. East from Kentucky to the nation's capital, causing hundreds of traffic accidents, knocking out power in places and threatening to flood waterways as temperatures began rising Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Early look: The views and venues of the 2026 Winter Olympics
item.description: The 2026 Winter Games will bring the world's best athletes to Northern Italy, with Milan and Cortina as key host cities, marking the first time in 20 years that the games return to Italy.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Orange, KRON4

item.title: White House says it has the right to punish AP reporters over Gulf naming dispute
item.description: The White House says news organizations that refuse to use President Donald Trump’s new name for the Gulf of Mexico are telling “lies.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says he expects to meet with Putin in Saudi Arabia
item.description: President Trump said Wednesday he expects to meet in person with Russian President Vladimir Putin multiple times, suggesting they are likely to meet first in Saudi Arabia. “We ultimately expect to meet,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dog seen riding in unsecured kennel along Indiana expressway now recovering
item.description: Video showed the dog in a kennel on a trailer that was being towed along an Indiana expressway in frigid temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KRON4

item.title: Pilot killed in collision involving Mötley Crüe singer's plane identified as Marine Corps veteran
item.description: Joie Vitosky, 78, was certified as a flight engineer since 1978 and a pilot since 2019, according to FAA records.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Barker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US eggs prices hit a record high of $4.95 and are likely to keep climbing
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Egg prices hit a record high as the U.S. contends with an ongoing bird flu outbreak, but consumers didn't need government figures released Wednesday to tell them eggs are terribly expensive and hard to find at times because of an ongoing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Your closed Party City may soon turn into one of these familiar stores
item.description: What used to be your local Party City store may soon be selling books, recliner chairs, cowboy boots or discount merchandise.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House Republicans unveil blueprint to advance Trump's agenda
item.description: House Republicans on Wednesday released a blueprint they intend to use to advance President Trump’s legislative agenda, breaking through an impasse that had stymied the conference for days.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Ozzy Osbourne announces 'final show' with original Black Sabbath
item.description: The performance, called "The Back to the Beginning," will cap off an all-day event featuring sets from several iconic heavy metal acts.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Belarus releases 3 people, including an American and a jailed journalist
item.description: U.S. officials say Belarus has released three people, including an American and a journalist, who’d been detained for more than three years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Military plane crashes near San Diego: authorities
item.description: Emergency crews are responding to reports of a military plane that crashed into the San Diego Bay near Shelter Island, according to the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US eggs prices hit a record high of $4.95 and are likely to keep climbing
item.description: Egg prices have reached a record high in the U.S. The consumer price index released on Wednesday confirmed what consumers, bakers and food companies already knew: an ongoing bird flu outbreak that began in 2022 are making eggs terribly expensive right now.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lawyers for Sonya Massey's family say $10M settlement 'first step;' await trial, legislative changes
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — A $10 million settlement between county officials in Springfield, Illinois, and the family of Sonya Massey is the “first step in getting full justice” for the Black woman fatally shot in her home last summer by a sheriff's deputy, lawyers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 homeless children freeze to death while sleeping in van with family in Detroit: police
item.description: Police in Detroit said that two homeless children froze to death while they slept in a van with their family on Monday, while the city’s mayor revealed that their mother had reached out to homeless services a few months ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell only Republican to vote against Gabbard
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) on Wednesday voted against confirming former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) to serve as the nation’s top intelligence official, sending a strong message to fellow GOP senators, some of whom privately doubted her qualifications to hold the job.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: When does a heartbeat start? South Carolina Supreme Court again takes up abortion issue
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — With a heartbeat abortion ban solidly in place in South Carolina, lawyers for the state and Planned Parenthood return to the state's highest court Wednesday to argue how restrictive the ban should be.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Government watchdogs fired by Trump sue his administration and ask a judge to reinstate them
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Eight government watchdogs have sued over their mass firing that removed oversight of President Donald Trump's new administration.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate confirms Gabbard to serve as nation’s top intelligence chief
item.description: The Senate voted largely along party lines Wednesday to confirm Tulsi Gabbard to serve as the director of national intelligence, the nation’s top intelligence official, despite strong objections from Democrats and initial misgivings from Republicans who questioned her experience and judgment.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: School gym roof collapses under 350 tons of snow
item.description: A recent snowstorm that pummeled parts of Oregon caused the roof at a high school gym to collapse under the weight of about 700,000 pounds of snow, school officials said.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KRON4

item.title: Audio from Titan submersible implosion released by NOAA
item.description: In the 23-second recording, the sound of static can be heard, followed by a booming noise — then more static.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Georgia school shooting suspect's father granted bond as victims share emotional statements in court
item.description: A Georgia judge granted Apalachee High School shooting suspect Colt Gray's father, Colin Gray, a $500,000 bond. Colin Gray has pleaded not guilty to 29 criminal counts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Education report reveals which states have seen biggest drops in reading, math scores
item.description: While some schools are starting to claw back some of the ground lost due to pandemic-era learning disruptions, but there's still a ways to go, a new report finds.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Cause of deadly Arizona plane crash revealed, deceased pilot identified
item.description: A landing gear failure caused the private jet owned by Vince Neil – the lead singer of Mötley Crüe – to crash into a parked private jet on Monday, killing its 78-year-old co-pilot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trader Joe's, Costco among grocers to limit how many eggs customers can buy
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Trader Joe’s and other grocers are limiting the number of eggs customers can buy across the U.S., citing limited supply caused by the ongoing bird flu outbreak. Trader Joe’s is capping purchases to one carton per customer each day, the Monrovia, California-based chain confirmed.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: PBS closes DEI office to comply with executive order
item.description: PBS' move comes as Trump's FCC also continues to turn up the heat on public broadcasters over taxpayer funding and editorial direction.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Canned tuna sold at Walmart, Trader Joe's, Costco, others is recalled in multiple states, including Illinois
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has announced a recall on canned tuna products sold at several different retailers including Walmart, Trader Joe's, Kroger, and Costco.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing at least 1 person, authorities say
item.description: At least one person was killed and others were injured when private jets collided Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5
